event.title: Community & Partnerships – EU & LATAM
event.abstract: In this presentation, I will discuss TomTom's engagement with open maps, open source, and open community collaboration models. I will focus specifically on our work in Belgium, as well as our broader activities across Europe and globally.

talks.title: geOrchestra: Spatial Data Infrastructure
talks.abstract: geOrchestra is a powerful open-source solution for managing and sharing geospatial data. It's a ready to use, complete, Spatial Data Infrastructure. geOrchestra is a software composed of well known open source components interconnected with each other: GeoNetwork, GeoServer, Mapstore and PostgreSQL with PostGIS.

talks.title: Mesurer l'accessibilité spatiale aux commerces bruxellois
talks.abstract: Nous proposons de présenter les résultats d'analyses qui ont porté sur l'accessibilité spatiale aux commerces du quotidien à travers les quartiers bruxellois, quantifiée par la distance moyenne à vol d'oiseau entre le lieu de domicile et les commerces les plus proches permettant des achats de nécessité courante (alimentation générale, pain, viande, pharmacie).

talks.title: State of GRASS GIS
talks.abstract: GRASS is one of the oldest free software geoprocessing tools, but it continues to enjoy steady development and improvement, and a healthy and joyous community. Even though it can be used as a full-fledged GIS suite, it is positioned more and more as a high-performance and scientifically sound geo processing engine.

talks.title: Supporting Mobility Decisions with MapStore
talks.abstract: MapStore is a powerful open-source product and framework for building, managing, and sharing web-based maps, dashboards, and geostories - directly in the browser. In this talk, Geo Solutions will share their experience with MapStore by presenting a real-world implementation for the Department of Mobility and Public Works (DMOW) of the Flemish Government.

talks.title: Spoorbereikbaarheid binnen de Vlaamse Ruit
talks.abstract: Een Zwitsers model, fijnmazig, efficiënt en rechtvaardig, dat is het spoorbeeld waar Vlaanderen naar wil evolueren. Maar de werkelijkheid toont een ander patroon: een netwerk met een duidelijke kern en een rafelige periferie. De Vlaamse Ruit, met haar stedelijke knooppunten en dichte stationsnetwerk, komt dicht in de buurt van die Zwitserse droom.

talks.title: Graph-driven Geospatial Analysis
talks.abstract: This presentation explores the fascinating intersection of geospatial analysis and graph theory through the lens of open source tools. We'll look at practical use cases, including routing with pgRouting, modeling and analyzing utility networks, ensuring topological consistency with PostGIS Topology and building spatial knowledge graphs with Neo4j.

talks.title: Cartostation: SIG web open source
talks.abstract: Nous ferons une rapide présentation de Cartostation et des grandes étapes de son développement, depuis sa création en 2016 jusqu'à maintenant (bientôt 10 ans !). Nous aborderons le contexte dans lequel ce logiciel est développé, et nous évoquerons les objectifs futurs liés à ce projet.

talks.title: Système d'information digitalisé AVEC
talks.abstract: La présentation traite de la mise en place d'un système d'information digitalisé pour le projet d'Inclusion Financière des membres des Associations Villageoises d'Épargne et de Crédit (AVEC) dans la région de la Nawa en Côte d'Ivoire.

talks.title: Géospatial avec Parquet & DuckDB
talks.abstract: Le format Parquet, bien connu dans les écosystèmes big data, gagne du terrain dans le monde géospatial grâce à son extension GeoParquet. Colonnaire, compressé, lisible en lecture partielle, il devient un format d'échange efficace pour les données géographiques très volumineuse.

talks.title: Dynamic Reporting with QGIS Server
talks.abstract: Creating a nice map for a dynamic report can be a lot of work with a mediocre result. This testcase explores how QGIS Server can be used to streamline and automate the map-making process. During a live demo, the setup of QGIS Server and the QGIS project will be shown.

talks.title: Field Data Collection with QField
talks.abstract: A talk about QField, the professional mobile data collection app for QGIS, with over 1.5 million downloads and 500K active users. Recognized as a Digital Public Good, QField supports essential UN Sustainable Development Goals, such as Clean Water, Sustainable Cities, Climate Action, and Life on Land.

talks.title: Proj4rs - Rust Implementation of PROJ
talks.abstract: Proj4rs open-source project has started because PROJ is linked to external C libraries such as sqlite3, so the reused of PROJ in rust is a binding with C libraries dependances and building to WASM is a pain. So we released Proj4rs, as a pure Rust implementation.

talks.title: Lizmap: Web Geoportal for QGIS
talks.abstract: Lizmap is an open-source web mapping application that works seamlessly with QGIS Server. Lizmap makes it easy to publish cartographic web applications. It can be used to create applications ranging from simple geographic data consultation to business applications, without any coding skills needed.

talks.title: ELAN: Nature-based Urban Water Management
talks.abstract: ELAN est un plugin QGIS d'aide à la décision développé par Oslandia à la demande de INRAE. Il a pour vocation d'accompagner la mise en œuvre d'une gestion durable et intégrée des eaux urbaines. ELAN permet d'identifier et de comparer différentes options de gestion des eaux.

talks.title: PostgreSQL Upgrade Strategies
talks.abstract: Upgrading PostgreSQL can feel like walking a tightrope, especially when PostGIS is involved. Many users put it off as long as possible, hoping the database gods will spare them from downtime or broken geospatial functions. But sooner or later, the upgrade must come. This talk is for developers, analysts, and GIS users who rely on PostgreSQL with PostGIS but don't wear a DBA badge. We will look at why upgrades matter, what you gain from doing them on time, and how to go about it without losing sleep.

talks.title: QJazz - Cloud-ready QGIS Server
talks.abstract: With our Lizmap hosting service, we provide and monitor several hundred Qgis Servers. In response to the difficulties we faced, we developed Py-QGIS-Server as an open-source project. But we needed to go one step further, to have a version of QGIS server perfectly adapted to the cloud. So we developped and released as open-source project: QJazz.

talks.title: Service Analysis with Openrouteservice
talks.abstract: This work presents a two-step method to analyse service provision (delivery, accessibility, emergency services, etc.). The aim is to test different positions of service centers to determine which combination of positions is the most efficient by assessing territorial coverage using isochrones.

talks.title: bePelias - Local Geocoding with BeSt Address
talks.abstract: Géocoder (standardiser + localiser) un grand volume d'adresses avec un outil commercial américain est un problème dans beaucoup de situations. D'une part parce que cela nécessite d'envoyer ses adresses en dehors de l'Europe, d'autre part parce que le résultat ne sera pas conforme à BeSt Address, le standard administratif de BOSA pour les adresses en Belgique. Avec bePelias, une version améliorée du géocodeur open-source Pelias adapté aux données de BeSt Address, nous proposons une solution on-premise de géocodage, ayant des performances comparables aux grandes solutions commerciales.

talks.title: Open-Source Data Platform with GeoNetwork
talks.abstract: With many new formats and protocols supported as well as powerful new UIs, GeoNetwork is going through a deep transformation. Its community is pushing it forward to become a first-class Open Source solution for data platforms. This means not only doing INSPIRE geospatial metadata but also exposing OGC API Records and DCAT-AP endpoints.

talks.title: brdr: Automated Geometry Alignment
talks.abstract: Het nauwkeurig afstemmen van geometrieën op referentiegegevens is een terugkerende uitdaging in geografische dataverwerking. De open-source Python-library brdr, ontwikkeld door Athumi en Agentschap Onroerend Erfgoed, biedt een krachtige en reproduceerbare oplossing voor dit probleem.

talks.title: Managing Geographic Data on OpenStreetMap
talks.abstract: We present Osmoa, a new tool for monitoring geographic data that is living on OpenStreetMap. Osmoa is tailored for organisations or companies that would like to use the power of the OSM community to enrich their thematic data while maintaining control and monitoring changes to their data.

talks.title: Metawal: Ready for DCAT-AP 3.0
talks.abstract: Présentation des travaux menés dans Metawal, le catalogue des données géographiques wallonnes basé sur GeoNetwork, pour supporter la conversion des métadonnées du standard ISO 19115-3 vers le standard DCAT-AP 3.0.

talks.title: QGIS Plugin for Statistical Analysis
talks.abstract: How to quickly create a QGIS plugin to help colleagues for different analyses. This presentation will focus on the steps involved in creating a QGIS plugin and then making it available to the public.

talks.title: Finding Geospatial Web Services
talks.abstract: A lot of geospatial data is readily available on the internet in webservices. But sometimes it's hard to find a webservice that has the data you need. 10 years ago Michel Stuyts started collecting lists of geospatial webservices that contain data for Belgium.

talks.title: OGC API Features & Processes Deep Dive
talks.abstract: Nederland en Belgie staan voor complexe maatschappelijke uitdagingen in de fysieke leefomgeving, zoals klimaatverandering en duurzame stadsontwikkeling. Technologieën als data-gedreven werken en Digitale Tweelingen worden gezien als kansrijke middelen om het beleidsproces effectiever te maken.

talks.title: Writing & Sharing QGIS Models
talks.abstract: The QGIS Model Designer offers a user-friendly interface for building geoprocessing workflows by chaining multiple operations—no programming skills required for using this great tool. But what happens when you want to share your models with others?

talks.title: Paragon: Open Source in Crisis Management
talks.abstract: Cette présentation n'a pas vocation technique, mais vise à partager un retour d'expérience sur les avantages stratégiques de l'open source et de l'open data dans les projets publics. À travers le cas concret du projet Paragon, avec une introduction présentant ce produit au service de la gestion de crise.
